description: Goldman Sachs is a global financial services firm centered on investment banking, markets, asset management, and wealth management for institutional, corporate, government, and high-net-worth clients.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Goldman Sachs operates as one of the world's most influential capital-markets firms, with core franchises in advisory, underwriting, trading, asset management, and wealth management. Its public identity is not built around consumer convenience so much as institutional reach, balance-sheet discipline, and the ability to be near the room when capital allocation decisions become expensive.

The firm reports around two central operating engines: Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management, with Platform Solutions treated as a smaller and strategically narrowed activity. For Free The World, Goldman Sachs is best understood less as a product company and more as financial infrastructure for corporations, investors, governments, funds, and wealthy households.

overview.title: Core Offerings
paragraphs: Global Banking & Markets combines investment banking advisory, debt and equity underwriting, financing, FICC, equities, prime services, and market-making. These services help clients raise capital, manage risk, execute transactions, and access liquidity, though the machinery is deliberately opaque enough that most ordinary people only notice it after a deal, crisis, or headline has already happened.

Asset & Wealth Management provides public and private market investment strategies, alternatives, advisory services, private banking, lending, and wealth planning. This business extends Goldman Sachs from episodic transaction work into recurring fee relationships with institutions, financial intermediaries, families, and individuals.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: Goldman Sachs belongs in the registry as a major incumbent in capital allocation, financial intermediation, and managed-investment services. The most relevant alternative-mapping areas are likely open finance tooling, decentralized capital markets infrastructure, transparent index and fund platforms, self-custody wealth tools, open research networks, and cooperative or public-interest banking models.

Because Goldman Sachs' products are mostly services, platforms, and institutional relationships rather than boxed software, comparisons should be framed around functions: advisory, underwriting, custody-like access, investment management, liquidity provision, risk analytics, and client reporting. The useful question is not whether an open alternative can become another Goldman Sachs; it is which slices of Goldman-style financial coordination can be made more transparent, accountable, and user-controlled.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs Investor Relations
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for annual reports, earnings materials, segment reporting, and management framing.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs 2025 Annual Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Most current annual-report source for business strategy, segment performance, risks, and shareholder-facing priorities.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs 2024 Annual Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful structured source for the firm's two main franchises: Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs What We Do / Our Businesses
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Official high-level description of the firm's current business lines and client-facing positioning.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs Serving Clients
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Explains how the firm presents its advisory, execution, and investment capabilities across client groups.

sourceSuggestions.title: Goldman Sachs Asset Management - About Us
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for Asset Management capabilities, investment strategies, client base, and alternatives positioning.

sourceSuggestions.title: SEC EDGAR - Goldman Sachs 2024 Form 10-K
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Regulatory filing with detailed business, risk, segment, legal, and financial disclosures suitable for source-backed registry claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: CompaniesMarketCap - Goldman Sachs Market Cap
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Market-cap reference already used in the manifest, helpful for index cohort and size context.
